name: Connie Francis
abstract: Concetta Rosa Maria Franconero ( FRAHNG-koh-NAIR-oh; December 12, 1937 – July 16, 2025), known professionally as Connie Francis, was an American singer and actress. One of the top-charting female vocalists of the late 1950s and early 1960s, she amassed over 200 million records sold, placing her among the best-selling music artists in his…

concerts.label: Connie Francis, Singer, and Special Guests

about_quotes.speaker: Fred Radke
about_quotes.evidence: The mention of Connie Francis occurs entirely within the [INTERVIEWER]'s turn. The [INTERVIEWEE] does not respond to or acknowledge this specific mention, nor does he mention Connie Francis in his own turns.
about_quotes.doc_title: Fred Radke interviewed by Monk Rowe, Hillsdale, Michigan, November 8, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Dan D'Imperio
about_quotes.evidence: Connie Francis was in that movie. I mean that was a movie. I mean they made a movie. I wasn’t — it was before I was with the band.

about_quotes.speaker: Bucky Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: And all Al would do is pick up the phone and call Connie Francis or somebody, “Hey we need a tenth song for the album.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Bucky Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Pallo
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I remember playing once with Connie Francis... [INTERVIEWEE] Same guy? Oh that’s great.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Pallo interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2007
